description: Fix critical security vulnerabilities in persona_loader.py eval() and soul.py directory traversal, plus cron.py shallow merge bug and concurrency issues
steps: In src/augur/persona_loader.py: Replace eval() in _eval_rule_condition() with AST-based safe evaluation. Use ast.parse(condition, mode='eval') and walk the AST to only allow Compare, BoolOp, BinOp, UnaryOp, Num/Constant, Name (from allowed namespace), and Attribute nodes. Reject any Call, Lambda, ListComp, SetComp, DictComp, GeneratorExp, or Attribute chains that access __class__, __bases__, __subclasses__, __builtins__, etc. This prevents arbitrary code execution while still supporting conditions like 'pe > 25' and 'roe > 0.15 and gross_margins > 0.4'.

In src/augur/soul.py inject_soul(): Add path sanitization. After constructing the output path, call .resolve() and verify it starts with out_dir.resolve(). Raise ValueError if directory traversal is detected. Also validate the format parameter - raise ValueError for unknown formats (only 'hermes', 'claude', 'raw' are valid).

In src/augur/cron.py load_watchlist(): Replace shallow dict.update() with a recursive deep-merge helper that preserves nested defaults. When user specifies schedule.cron but not schedule.timezone, the timezone default should be preserved.

In src/augur/cron.py start_scheduler(): Add max_instances=1 to the add_job() call to prevent overlapping runs. Also add a simple file-based lock mechanism using a pidfile at ~/.augur/scheduler.pid - check if another scheduler is running before starting.
In src/augur/cron.py: Remove unused 'import os' statement.

Add comprehensive tests in tests/test_iteration5.py covering: (a) eval sandbox rejects malicious payloads like class introspection, __import__, lambda; (b) eval sandbox allows legitimate conditions like 'pe > 25', 'roe > 0.15 and gross_margins > 0.4'; (c) inject_soul raises ValueError on path traversal attempt with '../'; (d) inject_soul raises ValueError on invalid format; (e) load_watchlist deep-merges nested config correctly preserving timezone defaults; (f) start_scheduler sets max_instances=1
acceptance_criteria: eval() in _eval_rule_condition is replaced with AST-based safe evaluation that blocks class introspection attacks
inject_soul raises ValueError when profile_path contains directory traversal sequences
inject_soul raises ValueError when format is not one of 'hermes', 'claude', 'raw'
load_watchlist correctly preserves nested defaults when user config is partial
start_scheduler uses max_instances=1 and checks for existing pidfile
Unused os import is removed from cron.py
All new tests pass and all existing 136 tests still pass
verification: python3 -m pytest tests/ -x -q (all tests pass including new test_iteration5.py)
python3 -c to confirm eval sandbox blocks malicious payloads
python3 -c to confirm path traversal is blocked
findings: All 161 tests pass (136 existing + 25 new). AST-based eval blocks the proven exploit. Path traversal and format validation work correctly. Deep merge preserves nested defaults. The os import in cron.py is now used by pidfile logic (os.kill, os.getpid).
